name: Dolmades Yialandzi (Stuffed Grapevine Leaves)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
2. Add the onion and scallions, sauté for about 5 minutes until soft and transparent.
3. Add the garlic and cook for a few minutes, then add the rice, dill, parsley, lemon juice, salt, pepper, and remaining 1/4 cup olive oil.
4. Stir well to combine all ingredients.
5. Add the hot water and bring to a simmer for about 5 minutes.
6. Remove from heat and let cool.
7. Carefully remove grapevine leaves from the jar, leaving the brine in the jar.
8. Wash grapevine leaves thoroughly and drain them, then with a sharp knife cut the heavy stems from the leaves (if using fresh leaves, parboil for 5 minutes before draining).
9. Line an enameled pan with a few heavy grapevine leaves.
10. To stuff a leaf, place it rough side up on your working surface with the stem end near you and add a teaspoonful of the rice mixture near the stem end.
11. Using both hands, fold the part of the leaf near you up and over the filling, then fold the right side of the leaf over the filling, followed by the left side, rolling tightly away from you toward the pointed end of the leaf.
12. Place the stuffed grapevine leaf (dolma) seam side down in the prepared pan.
13. Continue stuffing leaves until the mixture is used up. If any leaves remain, replace them in the reserved brine for future use.
14. Cover with an inverted plate and add enough water to cover the dolmades (about 1 to 1-1/2 cups).
15. Bring to a boil, then lower heat and simmer as slowly as possible for 1-1/4 hours.
16. Taste one to ensure the rice is tender; continue cooking if necessary.
17. Cool and chill before serving cold, either as an appetizer or entree.
Note: For a variation in Macedonia and Thrace, add raisins and pine nuts when adding the rice. Vary the size of dolmades by adding 1-1/2 teaspoons more filling, but ensure consistency for even cooking. Store in the refrigerator for up to a week.
